00065   static inline int ROUND_TO_LONG ( int n ) { return ((n+3)/4)*4; }

00075     const int length_;

00079   };

00089     const int length_;
00090

00093   };

00162     const int size_;

00167 
00174   class DATASTREAM {
00175     bool swap_;
00176     ::FILE* fp_;
00177 
00178     static bool bigEndian ( void );
00179   public:
00180     DATASTREAM ( ::FILE* fp = 0 ) : swap_( bigEndian() ), fp_( fp ) {}
00181 
00182     void setStream ( ::FILE* fp ) { fp_ = fp; }
00183 
00184     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const BYTE& byte )
00185     {
00186       fwrite( &byte, sizeof(BYTE), 1, fp_ );
00187       return *this;
00188     }
00189 
00190     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( BYTE& byte )
00191     {
00192       fread( &byte, sizeof(BYTE), 1, fp_ );
00193       return *this;
00194     }
00195 
00196     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const WORD& word )
00197     {
00198       if ( swap_ ) {
00199         unsigned char const * p = (unsigned char const*)&word;
00200         fwrite( &p[1], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00201         fwrite( &p[0], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00202       }
00203       else
00204         fwrite( &word, sizeof(WORD), 1, fp_ );
00205       return *this;
00206     }
00207 
00208     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( WORD& word )
00209     {
00210       if ( swap_ ) {
00211         unsigned char* p = (unsigned char*)&word;
00212         fread( &p[1], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00213         fread( &p[0], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00214       }
00215       else
00216         fread( &word, sizeof(WORD), 1, fp_ );
00217       return *this;
00218     }
00219 
00220     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const INT16& word )
00221     {
00222       if ( swap_ ) {
00223         unsigned char const * p = (unsigned char const*)&word;
00224         fwrite( &p[1], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00225         fwrite( &p[0], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00226       }
00227       else
00228         fwrite( &word, sizeof(INT16), 1, fp_ );
00229       return *this;
00230     }
00231 
00232     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( INT16& word )
00233     {
00234       if ( swap_ ) {
00235         unsigned char* p = (unsigned char*)&word;
00236         fread( &p[1], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00237         fread( &p[0], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00238       }
00239       else
00240         fread( &word, sizeof(INT16), 1, fp_ );
00241       return *this;
00242     }
00243 
00244     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const DWORD& dword )
00245     {
00246       if ( swap_ ) {
00247         unsigned char const* p = (unsigned char const*)&dword;
00248         fwrite( &p[3], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00249         fwrite( &p[2], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00250         fwrite( &p[1], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00251         fwrite( &p[0], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00252       }
00253       else
00254         fwrite( &dword, sizeof(DWORD), 1, fp_ );
00255       return *this;
00256     }
00257 
00258     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( DWORD& dword )
00259     {
00260       if ( swap_ ) {
00261         unsigned char* p = (unsigned char*)&dword;
00262         fread( &p[3], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00263         fread( &p[2], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00264         fread( &p[1], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00265         fread( &p[0], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00266       }
00267       else
00268         fread( &dword, sizeof(DWORD), 1, fp_ );
00269       return *this;
00270     }
00271 
00272     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const LONG& long_ )
00273     {
00274       if ( swap_ ) {
00275         unsigned char const* p = (unsigned char const*)&long_;
00276         fwrite( &p[3], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00277         fwrite( &p[2], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00278         fwrite( &p[1], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00279         fwrite( &p[0], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00280       }
00281       else
00282         fwrite( &long_, sizeof(LONG), 1, fp_ );
00283       return *this;
00284     }
00285 
00286     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( LONG& long_ )
00287     {
00288       if ( swap_ ) {
00289         unsigned char* p = (unsigned char*)&long_;
00290         fread( &p[3], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00291         fread( &p[2], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00292         fread( &p[1], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00293         fread( &p[0], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00294       }
00295       else
00296         fread( &long_, sizeof(LONG), 1, fp_ );
00297       return *this;
00298     }
00299 
00300     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const INT& int_ )
00301     {
00302       if ( swap_ ) {
00303         unsigned char const* p = (unsigned char const*)&int_;
00304         fwrite( &p[3], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00305         fwrite( &p[2], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00306         fwrite( &p[1], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00307         fwrite( &p[0], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00308       }
00309       else
00310         fwrite( &int_, sizeof(INT), 1, fp_ );
00311       return *this;
00312     }
00313 
00314     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( INT& int_ )
00315     {
00316       if ( swap_ ) {
00317         unsigned char* p = (unsigned char*)&int_;
00318         fread( &p[3], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00319         fread( &p[2], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00320         fread( &p[1], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00321         fread( &p[0], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00322       }
00323       else
00324         fread( &int_, sizeof(INT), 1, fp_ );
00325       return *this;
00326     }
00327 
00328     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const UINT& uint )
00329     {
00330       if ( swap_ ) {
00331         unsigned char const* p = (unsigned char const*)&uint;
00332         fwrite( &p[3], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00333         fwrite( &p[2], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00334         fwrite( &p[1], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00335         fwrite( &p[0], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00336       }
00337       else
00338         fwrite( &uint, sizeof(UINT), 1, fp_ );
00339       return *this;
00340     }
00341 
00342     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( UINT& uint )
00343     {
00344       if ( swap_ ) {
00345         unsigned char* p = (unsigned char*)&uint;
00346         fread( &p[3], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00347         fread( &p[2], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00348         fread( &p[1], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00349         fread( &p[0], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00350       }

00352         fread( &uint, sizeof(UINT), 1, fp_ );

00355 
00356     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const FLOAT& float_ )
00357     {
00358       if ( swap_ ) {
00359         unsigned char const* p = (unsigned char const*)&float_;
00360         fwrite( &p[3], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00361         fwrite( &p[2], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00362         fwrite( &p[1], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00363         fwrite( &p[0], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00364       }
00365       else
00366         fwrite( &float_, sizeof(FLOAT), 1, fp_ );
00367       return *this;
00368     }
00369 
00370     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( FLOAT& float_ )
00371     {
00372       if ( swap_ ) {
00373         unsigned char* p = (unsigned char*)&float_;
00374         fread( &p[3], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00375         fread( &p[2], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00376         fread( &p[1], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00377         fread( &p[0], sizeof(unsigned char), 1, fp_ );
00378       }
00379       else
00380         fread( &float_, sizeof(FLOAT), 1, fp_ );
00381       return *this;
00382     }
00383 
00384     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const PADDING& padding )
00385     {
00386       if ( padding.size_ != 0 )
00387         fwrite( &padding.padding_, sizeof(CHAR), padding.size_, fp_ );
00388       return *this;
00389     }
00390 
00391     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const RECTL& rectl )
00392     {
00393       *this << rectl.left << rectl.top << rectl.right << rectl.bottom;
00394       return *this;
00395     }
00396 
00397     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( RECTL& rectl )
00398     {
00399       *this >> rectl.left >> rectl.top >> rectl.right >> rectl.bottom;
00400       return *this;
00401     }
00402 
00403     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const SIZEL& sizel )
00404     {
00405       *this << sizel.cx << sizel.cy;
00406       return *this;
00407     }
00408 
00409     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( SIZEL& sizel )
00410     {
00411       *this >> sizel.cx >> sizel.cy;
00412       return *this;
00413     }
00414 
00415     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const WCHARSTR& wcharstr )
00416     {
00417       for ( int i = 0; i < wcharstr.length_; i++ )
00418         *this << wcharstr.string_[i];
00419       return *this;
00420     }
00421 
00422     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( WCHARSTR& wcharstr )
00423     {
00424       for ( int i = 0; i < wcharstr.length_; i++ )
00425         *this >> wcharstr.string_[i];
00426       return *this;
00427     }
00428 
00429     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const CHARSTR& charstr )
00430     {
00431       fwrite( charstr.string_, sizeof(CHAR), charstr.length_, fp_ );
00432       return *this;
00433     }
00434 
00435     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( CHARSTR& charstr )
00436     {
00437       fread( charstr.string_, sizeof(CHAR), charstr.length_, fp_ );
00438       return *this;
00439     }

00448     {

00460     {

00462       return *this;

00466     {

00489     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const XFORM& xform )
00490     {
00491       *this << xform.eM11 << xform.eM12 << xform.eM21 << xform.eM22
00492             << xform.eDx << xform.eDy;
00493       return *this;
00494     }
00495 
00496     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( XFORM& xform )
00497     {
00498       *this >> xform.eM11 >> xform.eM12 >> xform.eM21 >> xform.eM22
00499             >> xform.eDx >> xform.eDy;
00500       return *this;
00501     }
00502

00571     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const ::EMRTEXT& text )
00572     {
00573       *this << text.ptlReference << text.nChars << text.offString << text.fOptions
00574          << text.rcl << text.offDx;
00575       return *this;
00576     }
00577 
00578     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( ::EMRTEXT& text )
00579     {
00580       *this >> text.ptlReference >> text.nChars >> text.offString >> text.fOptions
00581             >> text.rcl >> text.offDx;
00582       return *this;
00583     }
00584 
00585     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const LOGPEN& pen )
00586     {
00587       *this << pen.lopnStyle << pen.lopnWidth << pen.lopnColor;
00588       return *this;
00589     }
00590 
00591     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( LOGPEN& pen )
00592     {
00593       *this >> pen.lopnStyle >> pen.lopnWidth >> pen.lopnColor;
00594       return *this;
00595     }
00596 
00597     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const EXTLOGPEN& pen )
00598     {
00599       // *** How big is this structure if there are no style entries? ***
00600       *this << pen.elpPenStyle << pen.elpWidth << pen.elpBrushStyle << pen.elpColor
00601             << pen.elpHatch << pen.elpNumEntries;
00602       return *this;
00603     }
00604 
00605     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( EXTLOGPEN& pen )
00606     {
00607       // *** How big is this structure if there are no style entries? ***
00608       *this >> pen.elpPenStyle >> pen.elpWidth >> pen.elpBrushStyle >> pen.elpColor
00609             >> pen.elpHatch >> pen.elpNumEntries;
00610       return *this;
00611     }
00612 
00613     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const LOGBRUSH& brush )
00614     {
00615       *this << brush.lbStyle << brush.lbColor << brush.lbHatch;
00616       return *this;
00617     }
00618 
00619     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( LOGBRUSH& brush )
00620     {
00621       *this >> brush.lbStyle >> brush.lbColor >> brush.lbHatch;
00622       return *this;
00623     }
00624 
00625     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const LOGFONTW& font )
00626     {
00627       *this << font.lfHeight << font.lfWidth << font.lfEscapement
00628             << font.lfOrientation << font.lfWeight << font.lfItalic
00629             << font.lfUnderline << font.lfStrikeOut << font.lfCharSet
00630             << font.lfOutPrecision << font.lfClipPrecision << font.lfQuality
00631             << font.lfPitchAndFamily
00632             << WCHARSTR( const_cast<WCHAR*const>(font.lfFaceName), LF_FACESIZE );
00633       return *this;
00634     }
00635 
00636     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( LOGFONTW& font )
00637     {
00638       WCHARSTR wFaceName( font.lfFaceName, LF_FACESIZE );
00639 
00640       *this >> font.lfHeight >> font.lfWidth >> font.lfEscapement
00641             >> font.lfOrientation >> font.lfWeight >> font.lfItalic
00642             >> font.lfUnderline >> font.lfStrikeOut >> font.lfCharSet
00643             >> font.lfOutPrecision >> font.lfClipPrecision >> font.lfQuality
00644             >> font.lfPitchAndFamily
00645             >> wFaceName;
00646       return *this;
00647     }
00648 
00649     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const PANOSE& panose )
00650     {
00651       fwrite( &panose, sizeof(PANOSE), 1, fp_ );
00652       return *this;
00653     }
00654 
00655     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( PANOSE& panose )
00656     {
00657       fread( &panose, sizeof(PANOSE), 1, fp_ );
00658       return *this;
00659     }
00660 
00661     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const EXTLOGFONTW& font )
00662     {
00663       *this << font.elfLogFont
00664             << WCHARSTR( const_cast<WCHAR*const>(font.elfFullName),
00665                          LF_FULLFACESIZE )
00666             << WCHARSTR( const_cast<WCHAR*const>(font.elfStyle), LF_FACESIZE )
00667             << font.elfVersion << font.elfStyleSize << font.elfMatch
00668             << font.elfReserved
00669             << BYTEARRAY( const_cast<BYTE*const>(font.elfVendorId),
00670                           ELF_VENDOR_SIZE )
00671             << font.elfCulture << font.elfPanose;
00672       return *this;
00673     }
00674 
00675     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( EXTLOGFONTW& font )
00676     {
00677       WCHARSTR wFullName( font.elfFullName, LF_FULLFACESIZE );
00678       WCHARSTR wStyle( font.elfStyle, LF_FACESIZE );
00679       BYTEARRAY bVendorId( font.elfVendorId, ELF_VENDOR_SIZE );
00680       *this >> font.elfLogFont
00681             >> wFullName >> wStyle
00682             >> font.elfVersion >> font.elfStyleSize >> font.elfMatch
00683             >> font.elfReserved >> bVendorId
00684             >> font.elfCulture >> font.elfPanose;
00685       return *this;
00686     }
00687 
00688     DATASTREAM& operator<< ( const LOGPALETTE& palette )
00689     {
00690       // *** How big is this structure if the palette is empty? ***
00691       *this << palette.palVersion << palette.palNumEntries;
00692       return *this;
00693     }
00694 
00695     DATASTREAM& operator>> ( LOGPALETTE& palette )
00696     {
00697       // *** How big is this structure if the palette is empty? ***
00698       *this >> palette.palVersion >> palette.palNumEntries;
00699       return *this;
00700     }
00701   };
00708   bool DATASTREAM::bigEndian ( void )
00709   {
00710     bool be16, be32;
00711     short ns = 0x1234;
00712     int nl = 0x12345678;
00713 
00714     unsigned char* p = (unsigned char*)&ns;
00715     be16 = *p == 0x12;
00716 
00717     p = (unsigned char*)&nl;
00718     if ( p[0] == 0x12 && p[1] == 0x34 && p[2] == 0x56 && p[3] == 0x78 )
00719       be32 = true;
00720     else if ( p[0] == 0x78 && p[1] == 0x56 && p[2] == 0x34 && p[3] == 0x12 )
00721       be32 = false;
00722     else
00723       be32 = !be16;
00724 
00725     if ( be32 != be16 ) {
00726       //      cerr << "endian-ness not consistent between short's and int's!" << endl;
00727       ::abort();
00728     }
00729 
00730     return be32;
00731   }
00732 
00733   class METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT;
00734 
00736 
00742   class METARECORD {
00743   public:
00750     virtual void execute ( METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT* source, HDC dc ) const = 0;
00757     virtual bool serialize ( DATASTREAM ds ) = 0;
00763     virtual int size ( void ) const = 0;
00769     virtual ~METARECORD( ) { }
00770 #ifdef ENABLE_EDITING
00771 
00775     virtual void edit ( void ) const {}
00776 #endif
00777   };
00778 
00779 #ifdef ENABLE_EDITING
00780   /* Miscellaneous editing routines */
00781   inline void edit_pen_style ( const char* tag, DWORD style )
00782   {
00783     printf( "\t%s : ", tag );
00784     switch ( style & PS_STYLE_MASK ) {
00785     case PS_SOLID: printf( "PS_SOLID" ); break;
00786     case PS_DASH: printf( "PS_DASH" ); break;
00787     case PS_DOT: printf( "PS_DOT" ); break;
00788     case PS_DASHDOT: printf( "PS_DASHDOT" ); break;
00789     case PS_DASHDOTDOT: printf( "PS_DASHDOTDOT" ); break;
00790     case PS_NULL: printf( "PS_NULL" ); break;
00791     case PS_INSIDEFRAME: printf( "PS_INSIDEFRAME" ); break;
00792     case PS_USERSTYLE: printf( "PS_USERSTYLE" ); break;
00793     case PS_ALTERNATE: printf( "PS_ALTERNATE" ); break;
00794     }
00795     switch ( style & PS_ENDCAP_MASK ) {
00796     case PS_ENDCAP_ROUND: printf( " | PS_ENDCAP_ROUND" ); break;
00797     case PS_ENDCAP_SQUARE: printf( " | PS_ENDCAP_SQUARE" ); break;
00798     case PS_ENDCAP_FLAT: printf( " | PS_ENDCAP_FLAT" ); break;
00799     }
00800     switch ( style & PS_JOIN_MASK ) {
00801     case PS_JOIN_ROUND: printf( " | PS_JOIN_ROUND" ); break;
00802     case PS_JOIN_BEVEL: printf( " | PS_JOIN_BEVEL" ); break;
00803     case PS_JOIN_MITER: printf( " | PS_JOIN_MITER" ); break;
00804     }
00805     switch ( style & PS_TYPE_MASK ) {
00806     case PS_COSMETIC: printf( " | PS_COSMETIC" ); break;
00807     case PS_GEOMETRIC: printf( " | PS_GEOMETRIC" ); break;
00808     }
00809     printf( "\n" );
00810   }
00811 
00812   inline void edit_brush_style ( const char* tag, DWORD style )
00813   {
00814     printf( "\t%s : ", tag );
00815     switch ( style ) {
00816     case BS_SOLID: printf( "BS_SOLID" ); break;
00817     case BS_NULL: printf( "BS_NULL" ); break;
00818     case BS_HATCHED: printf( "BS_HATCHED" ); break;
00819     case BS_PATTERN: printf( "BS_PATTERN" ); break;
00820     case BS_INDEXED: printf( "BS_INDEXED" ); break;
00821     case BS_DIBPATTERN: printf( "BS_DIBPATTERN" ); break;
00822     case BS_DIBPATTERNPT: printf( "BS_DIBPATTERNPT" ); break;
00823     case BS_PATTERN8X8: printf( "BS_PATTERN8X8" ); break;
00824     case BS_DIBPATTERN8X8: printf( "BS_DIBPATTERN8X8" ); break;
00825     case BS_MONOPATTERN: printf( "BS_DIBPATTERN8X8" ); break;
00826     default: printf( "unknown(%ld)", style );
00827     }
00828     printf( "\n" );
00829   }
00830 
00831   inline void edit_brush_hatch ( const char* tag, DWORD hatch )
00832   {
00833     printf( "\t%s : ", tag );
00834     switch ( hatch ) {
00835     case HS_HORIZONTAL: printf( "HS_HORIZONTAL" ); break;
00836     case HS_VERTICAL: printf( "HS_VERTICAL" ); break;
00837     case HS_FDIAGONAL: printf( "HS_FDIAGONAL" ); break;
00838     case HS_BDIAGONAL: printf( "HS_BDIAGONAL" ); break;
00839     case HS_CROSS: printf( "HS_CROSS" ); break;
00840     case HS_DIAGCROSS: printf( "HS_DIAGCROSS" ); break;
00841     default: printf( "unknown(%ld)", hatch );
00842     }
00843     printf( "\n" );
00844   }
00845 #endif
00846 
00853   enum OBJECTTYPE { O_METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT     = OBJ_METADC,
00854                     O_FONT                      = OBJ_FONT,
00855                     O_PEN                       = OBJ_PEN,
00856                     O_EXTPEN                    = OBJ_EXTPEN,
00857                     O_BRUSH                     = OBJ_BRUSH,
00858                     O_PALETTE                   = OBJ_PAL };
00859 #if 0
00860 
00863   static char* typStr ( OBJECTTYPE type )
00864   {
00865     switch (type) {
00866     case O_METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT:
00867       return "metafile device context";
00868     case O_FONT:
00869       return "font";
00870     case O_PEN:
00871       return "pen";
00872     case O_EXTPEN:
00873       return "extended pen";
00874     case O_BRUSH:
00875       return "brush";
00876     case O_PALETTE:
00877       return "palette";
00878     }
00879     return "unknown object";
00880   }
00881 #endif
00882 
00883 
00888   class OBJECT {
00889   public:
00890     HGDIOBJ handle;             
00891     virtual ~OBJECT () {}
00896     OBJECT ( void ) : handle( 0 ) {}
00900     virtual OBJECTTYPE getType ( void ) const = 0;
00901   };
00902 
00904 
00909   class GRAPHICSOBJECT : public OBJECT {
00910   public:
00911     virtual ~GRAPHICSOBJECT () {}
00916     std::map< HDC, HGDIOBJ > contexts;
00922     virtual METARECORD* newEMR ( HDC dc, HGDIOBJ handle ) = 0;
00923   };
00924 
00925   typedef METARECORD*(*METARECORDCTOR)(DATASTREAM&);
00926 
00927   class GLOBALOBJECTS {
00931     std::vector<OBJECT*> objects;
00932 
00939     std::map< DWORD, METARECORDCTOR > new_records;
00940 
00941   public:
00942     GLOBALOBJECTS ( void );
00943     ~GLOBALOBJECTS ( void );
00944     HGDIOBJ add ( OBJECT* object );
00945     OBJECT* find ( const HGDIOBJ handle );
00946     void remove ( const OBJECT* object );
00947 
00948     std::vector<EMF::OBJECT*>::const_iterator begin ( void ) const
00949     { return objects.begin(); }
00950     std::vector<EMF::OBJECT*>::const_iterator end ( void ) const
00951     { return objects.end(); }
00952 
00953     METARECORDCTOR newRecord ( DWORD iType ) const;
00954 
00955     static EMF::METARECORD* new_eof ( DATASTREAM& ds );
00956     static EMF::METARECORD* new_setviewportorgex ( DATASTREAM& ds );
00957     static EMF::METARECORD* new_setwindoworgex ( DATASTREAM& ds );
00958     static EMF::METARECORD* new_setviewportextex ( DATASTREAM& ds );
00959     static EMF::METARECORD* new_setwindowextex ( DATASTREAM& ds );
00960     static EMF::METARECORD* new_scaleviewportextex ( DATASTREAM& ds );
00961     static EMF::METARECORD* new_scalewindowextex ( DATASTREAM& ds );
00962     static EMF::METARECORD* new_modifyworldtransform ( DATASTREAM& ds );
00963     static EMF::METARECORD* new_setworldtransform ( DATASTREAM& ds );
00964     static EMF::METARECORD* new_settextalign ( DATASTREAM& ds );
00965     static EMF::METARECORD* new_settextcolor ( DATASTREAM& ds );
00966     static EMF::METARECORD* new_setbkcolor ( DATASTREAM& ds );
00967     static EMF::METARECORD* new_setbkmode ( DATASTREAM& ds );
00968     static EMF::METARECORD* new_setpolyfillmode ( DATASTREAM& ds );
00969     static EMF::METARECORD* new_setmapmode ( DATASTREAM& ds );
00970     static EMF::METARECORD* new_selectobject ( DATASTREAM& ds );
00971     static EMF::METARECORD* new_deleteobject ( DATASTREAM& ds );
00972     static EMF::METARECORD* new_movetoex ( DATASTREAM& ds );
00973     static EMF::METARECORD* new_lineto ( DATASTREAM& ds );
00974     static EMF::METARECORD* new_arc ( DATASTREAM& ds );
00975     static EMF::METARECORD* new_arcto ( DATASTREAM& ds );
00976     static EMF::METARECORD* new_rectangle ( DATASTREAM& ds );
00977     static EMF::METARECORD* new_ellipse ( DATASTREAM& ds );
00978     static EMF::METARECORD* new_polyline ( DATASTREAM& ds );
00979     static EMF::METARECORD* new_polyline16 ( DATASTREAM& ds );
00980     static EMF::METARECORD* new_polygon ( DATASTREAM& ds );
00981     static EMF::METARECORD* new_polypolygon ( DATASTREAM& ds );
00982     static EMF::METARECORD* new_polybezier ( DATASTREAM& ds );
00983     static EMF::METARECORD* new_polybezier16 ( DATASTREAM& ds );
00984     static EMF::METARECORD* new_polybezierto ( DATASTREAM& ds );
00985     static EMF::METARECORD* new_polybezierto16 ( DATASTREAM& ds );
00986     static EMF::METARECORD* new_polylineto ( DATASTREAM& ds );
00987     static EMF::METARECORD* new_polylineto16 ( DATASTREAM& ds );
00988     static EMF::METARECORD* new_exttextouta ( DATASTREAM& ds );
00989     static EMF::METARECORD* new_setpixelv ( DATASTREAM& ds );
00990     static EMF::METARECORD* new_createpen ( DATASTREAM& ds );
00991     static EMF::METARECORD* new_extcreatepen ( DATASTREAM& ds );
00992     static EMF::METARECORD* new_createbrushindirect ( DATASTREAM& ds );
00993     static EMF::METARECORD* new_extcreatefontindirectw ( DATASTREAM& ds );
00994     static EMF::METARECORD* new_fillpath ( DATASTREAM& ds );
00995     static EMF::METARECORD* new_strokepath ( DATASTREAM& ds );
00996     static EMF::METARECORD* new_beginpath ( DATASTREAM& ds );
00997     static EMF::METARECORD* new_endpath ( DATASTREAM& ds );
00998     static EMF::METARECORD* new_closefigure ( DATASTREAM& ds );
00999   };
01000 
01001   extern GLOBALOBJECTS globalObjects;
01002 
01004 
01010   class ENHMETAHEADER : public METARECORD, public ::ENHMETAHEADER {
01011 
01012     LPWSTR description_w;
01013     int description_size;
01014 
01015   public:
01022     ENHMETAHEADER ( LPCWSTR description = 0 )
01023       : description_w( 0 ), description_size( 0 )
01024     {
01025       iType = EMR_HEADER;
01026       nSize = sizeof( ::ENHMETAHEADER );
01027 
01028       // Compute the bounds
01029       RECTL default_bounds = { 0, 0, 0, 0 };
01030       rclBounds = default_bounds;
01031       RECTL default_frame = { 0, 0, 0, 0 };
01032       rclFrame = default_frame;
01033       dSignature = ENHMETA_SIGNATURE;
01034       nVersion = 0x10000;
01035       nBytes = nSize;
01036       nRecords = 1;
01037       nHandles = 0;
01038       sReserved = 0;
01039       nDescription = 0;
01040       offDescription = 0;
01041       nPalEntries = 0;
01042       szlDevice.cx = XMAX_PIXELS;
01043       szlDevice.cy = YMAX_PIXELS;
01044       szlMillimeters.cx = XMAX_MM;
01045       szlMillimeters.cy = YMAX_MM;
01046       //
01047       cbPixelFormat = 0;
01048       offPixelFormat = 0;
01049       bOpenGL = FALSE;
01050       //
01051       szlMicrometers.cx = 1000 * szlMillimeters.cx;
01052       szlMicrometers.cy = 1000 * szlMillimeters.cy;
01053 
01054       if ( description ) {
01055         // Count the number of characters in the description
01056         int description_count = 0, nulls = 0;
01057         LPCWSTR description_p = description;
01058         while ( nulls < 3 ) {
01059           description_count++;
01060           if ( (*description_p++) == 0 ) nulls++;
01061         }
01062 
01063         // Make sure that the TOTAL record length will be a multiple of 4
01064 
01065         int record_size = ROUND_TO_LONG( sizeof( ::ENHMETAHEADER ) +
01066                                          sizeof( WCHAR ) * description_count );
01067         description_size =
01068           (record_size - sizeof( ::ENHMETAHEADER )) / sizeof( WCHAR );
01069 
01070         description_w = new WCHAR[ description_size ];
01071 
01072         memset( description_w, 0, sizeof(WCHAR) * description_size );
01073 
01074         for ( int i=0; i<description_count; i++ )
01075           description_w[i] = *description++;
01076 
01077         nSize = nBytes = record_size;
01078         nDescription = description_count;
01079         offDescription = sizeof( ::ENHMETAHEADER );
01080       }
01081     }
01082     
01086     ~ENHMETAHEADER ( )
01087     {
01088       if ( description_w ) delete[] description_w;
01089     }
01094     bool serialize ( DATASTREAM ds )
01095     {
01096       ds << iType << nSize
01097          << rclBounds << rclFrame
01098          << dSignature << nVersion << nBytes << nRecords << nHandles << sReserved
01099          << nDescription << offDescription << nPalEntries
01100          << szlDevice << szlMillimeters
01101          << cbPixelFormat << offPixelFormat << bOpenGL
01102          << szlMicrometers
01103          << WCHARSTR( description_w, description_size );
01104       return true;
01105     }
01109     bool unserialize ( DATASTREAM ds )
01110     {
01111       ds >> iType >> nSize
01112          >> rclBounds >> rclFrame
01113          >> dSignature >> nVersion >> nBytes >> nRecords >> nHandles >> sReserved
01114          >> nDescription >> offDescription >> nPalEntries
01115          >> szlDevice >> szlMillimeters;
01116 
01117       // Some elements of the metafile header were added at later dates
01118 
01119 #define OffsetOf( a, b ) ((unsigned int)(((char*)&(((::ENHMETAHEADER*)a)->b)) - \
01120 (char*)((::ENHMETAHEADER*)a)))
01121 
01122       if ( OffsetOf( this, szlMicrometers ) <= offDescription )
01123         ds >> cbPixelFormat >> offPixelFormat >> bOpenGL;
01124 
01125 #undef OffsetOf
01126 
01127       if ( sizeof(::ENHMETAHEADER) <= offDescription )
01128         ds >> szlMicrometers;
01129 
01130       // Should now probably check that the offset is correct...
01131 
01132       description_size = ( nSize - offDescription ) / sizeof(WCHAR);
01133       description_w = new WCHAR[ description_size ];
01134 
01135       WCHARSTR description( description_w, description_size );
01136 
01137       ds >> description;
01138 
01139       return true;
01140     }
01144     int size ( void ) const { return nSize; }
01150     void execute ( METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT* source, HDC dc ) const
01151     {
01152       // Actually handled by the destination device context.
01153     }
01154 #ifdef ENABLE_EDITING
01155 
01158     void edit ( void ) const
01159     {
01160       printf( "*HEADER*\n" );
01161       printf( "\tiType             : %ld\n", iType );
01162       printf( "\tnSize             : %ld\n", nSize );
01163       printf( "\trclBounds.left    : %ld\n", rclBounds.left );
01164       printf( "\trclBounds.top     : %ld\n", rclBounds.top );
01165       printf( "\trclBounds.right   : %ld\n", rclBounds.right );
01166       printf( "\trclBounds.bottom  : %ld\n", rclBounds.bottom );
01167       printf( "\trclFrame.left     : %ld\n", rclFrame.left );
01168       printf( "\trclFrame.top      : %ld\n", rclFrame.top );
01169       printf( "\trclFrame.right    : %ld\n", rclFrame.right );
01170       printf( "\trclFrame.bottom   : %ld\n", rclFrame.bottom );
01171       printf( "\tdSignature        : %.4s\n", (const char*)&dSignature );
01172       printf( "\tnVersion          : 0x%x\n", (unsigned int)nVersion );
01173       printf( "\tnBytes            : %ld\n", nBytes );
01174       printf( "\tnRecords          : %ld\n", nRecords );
01175       printf( "\tnHandles          : %d\n", nHandles );
01176       printf( "\tnDescription      : %ld\n", nDescription );
01177       printf( "\toffDescription    : %ld\n", offDescription );
01178       printf( "\tnPalEntries       : %ld\n", nPalEntries );
01179       printf( "\tszlDevice.cx      : %ld\n", szlDevice.cx );
01180       printf( "\tszlDevice.cy      : %ld\n", szlDevice.cy );
01181       printf( "\tszlMillimeters.cx : %ld\n", szlMillimeters.cx );
01182       printf( "\tszlMillimeters.cy : %ld\n", szlMillimeters.cy );
01183 
01184       /* Make a crude guess as to the age of this file */
01185 #define OffsetOf( a, b ) ((unsigned int)(((const char*)&(((const ::ENHMETAHEADER*)a)->b)) - \
01186 (const char*)((const ::ENHMETAHEADER*)a)))
01187 
01188       if ( OffsetOf( this, cbPixelFormat ) <= offDescription ) {
01189         printf( "\tcbPixelFormat     : %ld\n", cbPixelFormat );
01190         printf( "\toffPixelFormat    : %ld\n", offPixelFormat );
01191         printf( "\tbOpenGL           : %ld\n", bOpenGL );
01192 
01193         if ( sizeof(::ENHMETAHEADER) <= offDescription ) {
01194           printf( "\tszlMicrometers.cx : %ld\n", szlMicrometers.cx );
01195           printf( "\tszlMicrometers.cy : %ld\n", szlMicrometers.cy );
01196         }
01197       }
01198 
01199 #undef OffsetOf
01200 
01201       if ( nDescription != 0 ) {
01202 
01203         wchar_t last_w = 0;
01204         WCHAR* description = description_w;
01205 
01206         printf( "\tDescription:" );
01207 
01208         for ( DWORD i = 0; i < nDescription; i++ ) {
01209 
01210           wchar_t w = *description++; /* This is not true, really. UNICODE is not
01211                                        * glibc's wide character representation */
01212 
01213           if ( w != 0 ) {
01214             if ( last_w == 0 ) printf( "\n\t\t" );
01215             putchar( w );
01216           }
01217           
01218           last_w = w;
01219         }
01220         printf( "\n" );
01221       }
01222     }
01223 #endif /* ENABLE_EDITING */
01224   };
01225 
01227 
01232   class EMREOF : public METARECORD, ::EMREOF {
01233   public:
01237     EMREOF ( void )
01238     {
01239       emr.iType = EMR_EOF;
01240       emr.nSize = sizeof( ::EMREOF );
01241       nPalEntries = 0;
01242       offPalEntries = 0;
01243       nSizeLast = 0;
01244     }
01245 
01250     EMREOF ( DATASTREAM& ds )
01251     {
01252       ds >> emr >> nPalEntries >> offPalEntries >> nSizeLast;
01253     }
01254 
01258     bool serialize ( DATASTREAM ds )
01259     {
01260       ds << emr << nPalEntries << offPalEntries << nSizeLast;
01261       return true;
01262     }
01266     int size ( void ) const { return emr.nSize; }
01272     void execute ( METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT* source, HDC dc ) const
01273     {
01274       // Actually handled by the destination device context.
01275     }
01276 #ifdef ENABLE_EDITING
01277 
01280     void edit ( void ) const
01281     {
01282       printf( "*EOF*\n" );
01283     }
01284 #endif /* ENABLE_EDITING */
01285   };
01286 
01288 
01293   class EMRSETVIEWPORTORGEX : public METARECORD, ::EMRSETVIEWPORTORGEX {
01294   public:
01299     EMRSETVIEWPORTORGEX ( INT x, INT y )
01300     {
01301       emr.iType = EMR_SETVIEWPORTORGEX;
01302       emr.nSize = sizeof( ::EMRSETVIEWPORTORGEX );
01303       ptlOrigin.x = x;
01304       ptlOrigin.y = y;
01305     }
01310     EMRSETVIEWPORTORGEX ( DATASTREAM& ds )
01311     {
01312       ds >> emr >> ptlOrigin;
01313     }
01317     bool serialize ( DATASTREAM ds )
01318     {
01319       ds << emr << ptlOrigin;
01320       return true;
01321     }
01325     int size ( void ) const { return emr.nSize; }
01331     void execute ( METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT* source, HDC dc ) const
01332     {
01333       SetViewportOrgEx( dc, ptlOrigin.x, ptlOrigin.y, 0 );
01334     }
01335 #ifdef ENABLE_EDITING
01336 
01339     void edit ( void ) const
01340     {
01341       printf( "*SETVIEWPORTORGEX*\n" );
01342       printf( "\tptlOrigin: %ld, %ld\n", ptlOrigin.x, ptlOrigin.y );
01343     }
01344 #endif /* ENABLE_EDITING */
01345   };
01346 
01348

01394     {

01396     }

01404       printf( "\tptlOrigin: %ld, %ld\n", ptlOrigin.x, ptlOrigin.y );

01407   };

01422     {
01423       emr.iType = EMR_SETVIEWPORTEXTEX;

01426       szlExtent.cy = cy;

01470 
01475   class EMRSCALEVIEWPORTEXTEX : public METARECORD, ::EMRSCALEVIEWPORTEXTEX {
01476   public:
01483     EMRSCALEVIEWPORTEXTEX ( LONG x_num, LONG x_den, LONG y_num, LONG y_den )
01484     {
01485       emr.iType = EMR_SCALEVIEWPORTEXTEX;
01486       emr.nSize = sizeof( ::EMRSCALEVIEWPORTEXTEX );
01487       xNum = x_num;
01488       xDenom = x_den;
01489       yNum = y_num;
01490       yDenom = y_den;
01491     }
01496     EMRSCALEVIEWPORTEXTEX ( DATASTREAM& ds )
01497     {
01498       ds >> emr >> xNum >> xDenom >> yNum >> yDenom;
01499     }
01503     bool serialize ( DATASTREAM ds )
01504     {
01505       ds << emr << xNum << xDenom << yNum << yDenom;
01506       return true;
01507     }
01511     int size ( void ) const { return emr.nSize; }
01517     void execute ( METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT* source, HDC dc ) const
01518     {
01519       ScaleViewportExtEx( dc, xNum, xDenom, yNum, yDenom, 0 );
01520     }
01521 #ifdef ENABLE_EDITING
01522 
01525     void edit ( void ) const
01526     {
01527       printf( "*SCALEVIEWPORTEXTEX*\n" );
01528       printf( "\txNum: %ld\n", xNum );
01529       printf( "\txDenom: %ld\n", xDenom );
01530       printf( "\tyNum: %ld\n", yNum );
01531       printf( "\tyDenom: %ld\n", yDenom );
01532     }
01533 #endif /* ENABLE_EDITING */
01534   };

01615       xDenom = x_den;
01616       yNum = y_num;

01626     }

01655       printf( "\txNum: %ld\n", xNum );
01656       printf( "\txDenom: %ld\n", xDenom );
01657       printf( "\tyNum: %ld\n", yNum );

01670   class EMRMODIFYWORLDTRANSFORM : public METARECORD, ::EMRMODIFYWORLDTRANSFORM {
01671   public:
01677     EMRMODIFYWORLDTRANSFORM ( const XFORM* transform, DWORD mode )
01678     {
01679       emr.iType = EMR_MODIFYWORLDTRANSFORM;
01680       emr.nSize = sizeof( ::EMRMODIFYWORLDTRANSFORM );
01681       xform = *transform;
01682       iMode = mode;
01683     }
01688     EMRMODIFYWORLDTRANSFORM ( DATASTREAM& ds )
01689     {
01690       ds >> emr >> xform >> iMode;
01691     }
01695     bool serialize ( DATASTREAM ds )
01696     {
01697       ds << emr << xform << iMode;
01698       return true;
01699     }
01703     int size ( void ) const { return emr.nSize; }
01709     void execute ( METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT* source, HDC dc ) const
01710     {
01711       ModifyWorldTransform( dc, &xform, iMode );
01712     }
01713 #ifdef ENABLE_EDITING
01714 
01717     void edit ( void ) const
01718     {
01719       printf( "*MODIFYWORLDTRANSFORM*\n" );
01720       printf( "\txform.eM11  : %f\n", xform.eM11 );
01721       printf( "\txform.eM12  : %f\n", xform.eM12 );
01722       printf( "\txform.eM21  : %f\n", xform.eM21 );
01723       printf( "\txform.eM22  : %f\n", xform.eM22 );
01724       printf( "\txform.eDx   : %f\n", xform.eDx );
01725       printf( "\txform.eDy   : %f\n", xform.eDy );
01726       printf( "\tiMode: " );
01727       switch ( iMode ) {
01728       case MWT_IDENTITY: printf( "MWT_IDENTITY\n" ); break;
01729       case MWT_LEFTMULTIPLY: printf( "MWT_LEFTMULTIPLY\n" ); break;
01730       case MWT_RIGHTMULTIPLY: printf( "MWT_RIGHTMULTIPLY\n" ); break;
01731       default: printf( "unknown(%ld)\n", iMode );
01732       }
01733     }
01734 #endif /* ENABLE_EDITING */
01735   };
01736 
01738

01753       xform = *transform;
01754     }

01760     {
01761       ds >> emr >> xform;
01762     }

01767     {
01768       ds << emr << xform;
01769       return true;
01770     }

01781     {
01782       SetWorldTransform( dc, &xform );
01783     }
01784 #ifdef ENABLE_EDITING

01788     void edit ( void ) const

01791       printf( "\txform.eM11  : %f\n", xform.eM11 );
01792       printf( "\txform.eM12  : %f\n", xform.eM12 );
01793       printf( "\txform.eM21  : %f\n", xform.eM21 );
01794       printf( "\txform.eM22  : %f\n", xform.eM22 );
01795       printf( "\txform.eDx   : %f\n", xform.eDx );

01802 
01805   class EMRSETTEXTALIGN : public METARECORD, ::EMRSETTEXTALIGN {
01806   public:
01810     EMRSETTEXTALIGN ( UINT mode )
01811     {
01812       emr.iType = EMR_SETTEXTALIGN;
01813       emr.nSize = sizeof( ::EMRSETTEXTALIGN );
01814       iMode = mode;
01815     }
01820     EMRSETTEXTALIGN ( DATASTREAM& ds )
01821     {
01822       ds >> emr >> iMode;
01823     }
01827     bool serialize ( DATASTREAM ds )
01828     {
01829       ds << emr << iMode;
01830       return true;
01831     }
01835     int size ( void ) const { return emr.nSize; }
01841     void execute ( METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT* source, HDC dc ) const
01842     {
01843       SetTextAlign( dc, iMode );
01844     }
01845 #ifdef ENABLE_EDITING
01846 
01849     void edit ( void ) const
01850     {
01851       unsigned int known_bits = TA_BASELINE+TA_CENTER+TA_UPDATECP+TA_RTLREADING;
01852       unsigned int unknown_bits = ~known_bits;
01853 
01854       printf( "*SETTEXTALIGN*\n" );
01855       printf( "\tiMode: " );
01856       if ( iMode & TA_UPDATECP )
01857         printf( "TA_UPDATECP" );
01858       else
01859         printf( "TA_NOUPDATECP" );
01860       if ( iMode & TA_CENTER )
01861         printf( " | TA_CENTER" );
01862       else if ( iMode & TA_RIGHT )
01863         printf( " | TA_RIGHT" );
01864       else
01865         printf( " | TA_LEFT" );
01866       if ( iMode & TA_BASELINE )
01867         printf( " | TA_BASELINE" );
01868       else if ( iMode & TA_BOTTOM )
01869         printf( " | TA_BOTTOM" );
01870       else
01871         printf( " | TA_TOP" );
01872       if ( iMode & TA_RTLREADING )
01873         printf( " | TA_RTLREADING" );
01874       if ( iMode & unknown_bits )
01875         printf( " | unknown bits(0x%lx)", iMode & unknown_bits );
01876       printf( "\n" );
01877     }
01878 #endif /* ENABLE_EDITING */
01879   };
01880 
01882

02410   class EMRARC : public METARECORD, ::EMRARC {
02411   public:
02423     EMRARC ( INT left, INT top, INT right, INT bottom, INT xstart,
02424              INT ystart, INT xend, INT yend )
02425     {
02426       emr.iType = EMR_ARC;
02427       emr.nSize = sizeof( ::EMRARC );
02428       rclBox.left = left;
02429       rclBox.right = right;
02430       rclBox.bottom = bottom;
02431       rclBox.top = top;
02432       ptlStart.x = xstart;
02433       ptlStart.y = ystart;
02434       ptlEnd.x = xend;
02435       ptlEnd.y = yend;
02436     }
02441     EMRARC ( DATASTREAM& ds )
02442     {
02443       ds >> emr >> rclBox >> ptlStart >> ptlEnd;
02444     }
02448     bool serialize ( DATASTREAM ds )
02449     {
02450       ds << emr << rclBox << ptlStart << ptlEnd;
02451       return true;
02452     }
02456     int size ( void ) const { return emr.nSize; }
02462     void execute ( METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT* source, HDC dc ) const
02463     {
02464       Arc( dc, rclBox.left, rclBox.top, rclBox.right, rclBox.bottom,
02465            ptlStart.x, ptlStart.y, ptlEnd.x, ptlEnd.y );
02466     }
02467 #ifdef ENABLE_EDITING
02468 
02471     void edit ( void ) const
02472     {
02473       printf( "*ARC*\n" );
02474       printf( "\trclBox.left    : %ld\n", rclBox.left );
02475       printf( "\trclBox.top     : %ld\n", rclBox.top );
02476       printf( "\trclBox.right   : %ld\n", rclBox.right );
02477       printf( "\trclBox.bottom  : %ld\n", rclBox.bottom );
02478       printf( "\tptlStart.x     : %ld\n", ptlStart.x );
02479       printf( "\tptlStart.y     : %ld\n", ptlStart.y );
02480       printf( "\tptlEnd.x       : %ld\n", ptlEnd.x );
02481       printf( "\tptlEnd.y       : %ld\n", ptlEnd.y );
02482     }
02483 #endif /* ENABLE_EDITING */
02484   };
02485 
02487 
02490   class EMRARCTO : public METARECORD, ::EMRARCTO {
02491   public:
02503     EMRARCTO ( INT left, INT top, INT right, INT bottom, INT xstart,
02504              INT ystart, INT xend, INT yend )
02505     {
02506       emr.iType = EMR_ARCTO;
02507       emr.nSize = sizeof( ::EMRARCTO );
02508       rclBox.left = left;
02509       rclBox.right = right;
02510       rclBox.bottom = bottom;
02511       rclBox.top = top;
02512       ptlStart.x = xstart;
02513       ptlStart.y = ystart;
02514       ptlEnd.x = xend;
02515       ptlEnd.y = yend;
02516     }
02521     EMRARCTO ( DATASTREAM& ds )
02522     {
02523       ds >> emr >> rclBox >> ptlStart >> ptlEnd;
02524     }
02528     bool serialize ( DATASTREAM ds )
02529     {
02530       ds << emr << rclBox << ptlStart << ptlEnd;
02531       return true;
02532     }
02536     int size ( void ) const { return emr.nSize; }
02542     void execute ( METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT* source, HDC dc ) const
02543     {
02544       ArcTo( dc, rclBox.left, rclBox.top, rclBox.right, rclBox.bottom,
02545              ptlStart.x, ptlStart.y, ptlEnd.x, ptlEnd.y );
02546     }
02547 #ifdef ENABLE_EDITING
02548 
02551     void edit ( void ) const
02552     {
02553       printf( "*ARCTO*\n" );
02554       printf( "\trclBox.left    : %ld\n", rclBox.left );
02555       printf( "\trclBox.top     : %ld\n", rclBox.top );
02556       printf( "\trclBox.right   : %ld\n", rclBox.right );
02557       printf( "\trclBox.bottom  : %ld\n", rclBox.bottom );
02558       printf( "\tptlStart.x     : %ld\n", ptlStart.x );
02559       printf( "\tptlStart.y     : %ld\n", ptlStart.y );
02560       printf( "\tptlEnd.x       : %ld\n", ptlEnd.x );
02561       printf( "\tptlEnd.y       : %ld\n", ptlEnd.y );
02562     }
02563 #endif /* ENABLE_EDITING */
02564   };
02565 
02567 
02570   class EMRRECTANGLE : public METARECORD, ::EMRRECTANGLE {
02571   public:
02578     EMRRECTANGLE ( INT left, INT top, INT right, INT bottom )
02579     {
02580       emr.iType = EMR_RECTANGLE;
02581       emr.nSize = sizeof( ::EMRRECTANGLE );
02582       rclBox.left = left;
02583       rclBox.right = right;
02584       rclBox.bottom = bottom;
02585       rclBox.top = top;
02586     }
02591     EMRRECTANGLE ( DATASTREAM& ds )
02592     {
02593       ds >> emr >> rclBox;
02594     }
02598     bool serialize ( DATASTREAM ds )
02599     {
02600       ds << emr << rclBox;
02601       return true;
02602     }
02606     int size ( void ) const { return emr.nSize; }
02612     void execute ( METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT* source, HDC dc ) const
02613     {
02614       Rectangle( dc, rclBox.left, rclBox.top, rclBox.right, rclBox.bottom );
02615     }
02616 #ifdef ENABLE_EDITING
02617 
02620     void edit ( void ) const
02621     {
02622       printf( "*RECTANGLE*\n" );
02623       printf( "\trclBox.left    : %ld\n", rclBox.left );
02624       printf( "\trclBox.top     : %ld\n", rclBox.top );
02625       printf( "\trclBox.right   : %ld\n", rclBox.right );
02626       printf( "\trclBox.bottom  : %ld\n", rclBox.bottom );
02627     }
02628 #endif /* ENABLE_EDITING */
02629   };
02630 
02632 
02635   class EMRELLIPSE : public METARECORD, ::EMRELLIPSE {
02636   public:
02644     EMRELLIPSE ( INT left, INT top, INT right, INT bottom )
02645     {
02646       emr.iType = EMR_ELLIPSE;
02647       emr.nSize = sizeof( ::EMRELLIPSE );
02648       rclBox.left = left;
02649       rclBox.right = right;
02650       rclBox.bottom = bottom;
02651       rclBox.top = top;
02652     }
02657     EMRELLIPSE ( DATASTREAM& ds )
02658     {
02659       ds >> emr >> rclBox;
02660     }
02664     bool serialize ( DATASTREAM ds )
02665     {
02666       ds << emr << rclBox;
02667       return true;
02668     }
02672     int size ( void ) const { return emr.nSize; }
02678     void execute ( METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT* source, HDC dc ) const
02679     {
02680       Ellipse( dc, rclBox.left, rclBox.top, rclBox.right, rclBox.bottom );
02681     }
02682 #ifdef ENABLE_EDITING
02683 
02686     void edit ( void ) const
02687     {
02688       printf( "*ELLIPSE*\n" );
02689       printf( "\trclBox.left    : %ld\n", rclBox.left );
02690       printf( "\trclBox.top     : %ld\n", rclBox.top );
02691       printf( "\trclBox.right   : %ld\n", rclBox.right );
02692       printf( "\trclBox.bottom  : %ld\n", rclBox.bottom );
02693     }
02694 #endif /* ENABLE_EDITING */
02695   };
02696 
02698 
02701   class EMRPOLYLINE : public METARECORD, ::EMRPOLYLINE {
02702     POINTL* lpoints;
02703   public:
02709     EMRPOLYLINE ( const RECTL* bounds, const POINT* points, INT n )
02710     {
02711       cptl = n;
02712       aptl[0].x = 0;            // Really unused
02713       aptl[0].y = 0;
02714 
02715       emr.iType = EMR_POLYLINE;
02716       // The (cptl - 1) below is to account for aptl, which isn't written out
02717       emr.nSize = sizeof( ::EMRPOLYLINE ) + sizeof( POINTL ) * ( cptl - 1);
02718 
02719       lpoints = new POINTL[cptl];
02720 
02721       for (int i=0; i<n; i++) {
02722         lpoints[i].x = points[i].x;
02723         lpoints[i].y = points[i].y;
02724       }
02725 
02726       rclBounds = *bounds;
02727     }
02731     ~EMRPOLYLINE ( )
02732     {
02733       if ( lpoints ) delete[] lpoints;
02734     }
02739     EMRPOLYLINE ( DATASTREAM& ds )
02740     {
02741       ds >> emr >> rclBounds >> cptl;
02742 
02743       lpoints = new POINTL[cptl];
02744 
02745       POINTLARRAY points( lpoints, cptl );
02746 
02747       ds >> points;
02748     }
02752     bool serialize ( DATASTREAM ds )
02753     {
02754       ds << emr << rclBounds << cptl << POINTLARRAY( lpoints, cptl );
02755       return true;
02756     }
02760     int size ( void ) const { return emr.nSize; }
02766     void execute ( METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT* source, HDC dc ) const
02767     {
02768       // According to the wine windef.h header, POINT and POINTL are equivalent
02769       Polyline( dc, (POINT*)lpoints, cptl );
02770     }
02771 #ifdef ENABLE_EDITING
02772 
02775     void edit ( void ) const
02776     {
02777       printf( "*POLYLINE*\n" );
02778       printf( "\trclBounds.left    : %ld\n", rclBounds.left );
02779       printf( "\trclBounds.top     : %ld\n", rclBounds.top );
02780       printf( "\trclBounds.right   : %ld\n", rclBounds.right );
02781       printf( "\trclBounds.bottom  : %ld\n", rclBounds.bottom );
02782       printf( "\tcptl              : %ld\n", cptl );
02783       printf( "\taptl->\n" );
02784       for ( unsigned int i = 0; i < cptl; i++ )
02785         printf( "\t\t%ld, %ld\n", lpoints[i].x, lpoints[i].y );
02786     }
02787 #endif /* ENABLE_EDITING */
02788   };
02789 
02791 
02794   class EMRPOLYLINE16 : public METARECORD, ::EMRPOLYLINE16 {
02795     POINT16* lpoints;
02796   public:
02802     EMRPOLYLINE16 ( const RECTL* bounds, const POINT16* points, INT n )
02803     {
02804       cpts = n;
02805       apts[0].x = 0;            // Really unused
02806       apts[0].y = 0;
02807 
02808       emr.iType = EMR_POLYLINE16;
02809       // The (cptl - 1) below is to account for aptl, which isn't written out
02810       emr.nSize = sizeof( ::EMRPOLYLINE16 ) + sizeof( POINT16 ) * ( cpts - 1);
02811 
02812       lpoints = new POINT16[cpts];
02813 
02814       for (int i=0; i<n; i++) {
02815         lpoints[i].x = points[i].x;
02816         lpoints[i].y = points[i].y;
02817       }
02818 
02819       rclBounds = *bounds;
02820     }
02824     ~EMRPOLYLINE16 ( )
02825     {
02826       if ( lpoints ) delete[] lpoints;
02827     }
02832     EMRPOLYLINE16 ( DATASTREAM& ds )
02833     {
02834       ds >> emr >> rclBounds >> cpts;
02835 
02836       lpoints = new POINT16[cpts];
02837 
02838       POINT16ARRAY points( lpoints, cpts );
02839 
02840       ds >> points;
02841     }
02845     bool serialize ( DATASTREAM ds )
02846     {
02847       ds << emr << rclBounds << cpts << POINT16ARRAY( lpoints, cpts );
02848       return true;
02849     }
02853     int size ( void ) const { return emr.nSize; }
02859     void execute ( METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT* source, HDC dc ) const
02860     {
02861       // According to the wine windef.h header, POINT and POINTL are equivalent
02862       Polyline16( dc, lpoints, cpts );
02863     }
02864 #ifdef ENABLE_EDITING
02865 
02868     void edit ( void ) const
02869     {
02870       printf( "*POLYLINE16*\n" );
02871       printf( "\trclBounds.left    : %ld\n", rclBounds.left );
02872       printf( "\trclBounds.top     : %ld\n", rclBounds.top );
02873       printf( "\trclBounds.right   : %ld\n", rclBounds.right );
02874       printf( "\trclBounds.bottom  : %ld\n", rclBounds.bottom );
02875       printf( "\tcpts              : %ld\n", cpts );
02876       printf( "\tapts->\n" );
02877       for ( unsigned int i = 0; i < cpts; i++ )
02878         printf( "\t\t%d, %d\n", lpoints[i].x, lpoints[i].y );
02879     }
02880 #endif /* ENABLE_EDITING */
02881   };
02882 
02884 
02887   class EMRPOLYGON : public METARECORD, ::EMRPOLYGON {
02888     POINTL* lpoints;
02889   public:
02895     EMRPOLYGON ( const RECTL* bounds, const POINT* points, INT n )
02896     {
02897       cptl = n;
02898       aptl[0].x = 0;            // Really unused
02899       aptl[0].y = 0;
02900 
02901       emr.iType = EMR_POLYGON;
02902       // The (cptl-1) below is to account for aptl, which isn't written out
02903       emr.nSize = sizeof( ::EMRPOLYGON ) + sizeof( POINTL ) * (cptl-1);
02904 
02905       lpoints = new POINTL[cptl];
02906 
02907       for (int i=0; i<n; i++) {
02908         lpoints[i].x = points[i].x;
02909         lpoints[i].y = points[i].y;
02910       }
02911 
02912       rclBounds = *bounds;
02913     }
02918     EMRPOLYGON ( DATASTREAM& ds )
02919     {
02920       ds >> emr >> rclBounds >> cptl;
02921 
02922       lpoints = new POINTL[cptl];
02923 
02924       POINTLARRAY points( lpoints, cptl );
02925 
02926       ds >> points;
02927     }
02931     ~EMRPOLYGON ( )
02932     {
02933       if ( lpoints ) delete[] lpoints;
02934     }
02938     bool serialize ( DATASTREAM ds )
02939     {
02940       ds << emr << rclBounds << cptl << POINTLARRAY( lpoints, cptl );
02941       return true;
02942     }
02946     int size ( void ) const { return emr.nSize; }
02952     void execute ( METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT* source, HDC dc ) const
02953     {
02954       // According to the wine windef.h header, POINT and POINTL are equivalent
02955       Polygon( dc, (POINT*)lpoints, cptl );
02956     }
02957 #ifdef ENABLE_EDITING
02958 
02961     void edit ( void ) const
02962     {
02963       printf( "*POLYGON*\n" );
02964       printf( "\trclBounds.left    : %ld\n", rclBounds.left );
02965       printf( "\trclBounds.top     : %ld\n", rclBounds.top );
02966       printf( "\trclBounds.right   : %ld\n", rclBounds.right );
02967       printf( "\trclBounds.bottom  : %ld\n", rclBounds.bottom );
02968       printf( "\tcptl              : %ld\n", cptl );
02969       printf( "\taptl->\n" );
02970       for ( unsigned int i = 0; i < cptl; i++ )
02971         printf( "\t\t%ld, %ld\n", lpoints[i].x, lpoints[i].y );
02972     }
02973 #endif /* ENABLE_EDITING */
02974   };
02975 
02977 
02980   class EMRPOLYPOLYGON : public METARECORD, ::EMRPOLYPOLYGON {
02981     DWORD* lcounts;
02982     POINTL* lpoints;
02983   public:
02990     EMRPOLYPOLYGON ( const RECTL* bounds, const POINT* points, const INT* counts,
02991                      UINT polygons )
02992     {
02993       nPolys = polygons;
02994       // Count the number of points in points
02995       int n = 0;
02996       for ( unsigned int i = 0; i < nPolys; i++ )
02997         n += counts[i];
02998 
02999       cptl = n;
03000       aPolyCounts[0] = 0;       // Really unused
03001       aptl[0].x = 0;
03002       aptl[0].y = 0;
03003 
03004       emr.iType = EMR_POLYPOLYGON;
03005       // The (#-1)'s below are to account for aPolyCounts[0] and aptl[0], which
03006       // aren't directly written out
03007       emr.nSize = sizeof( ::EMRPOLYPOLYGON ) + sizeof( POINTL ) * (cptl-1)
03008         + sizeof( DWORD ) * (nPolys-1);
03009 
03010       lcounts = new DWORD[nPolys];
03011 
03012       for ( unsigned int i = 0; i < nPolys; i++ )
03013         lcounts[i] = counts[i];
03014 
03015       lpoints = new POINTL[cptl];
03016 
03017       for (int i=0; i<n; i++) {
03018         lpoints[i].x = points[i].x;
03019         lpoints[i].y = points[i].y;
03020       }
03021 
03022       rclBounds = *bounds;
03023     }
03027     ~EMRPOLYPOLYGON ( )
03028     {
03029       if ( lcounts ) delete[] lcounts;
03030       if ( lpoints ) delete[] lpoints;
03031     }
03036     EMRPOLYPOLYGON ( DATASTREAM& ds )
03037     {
03038       ds >> emr >> rclBounds >> nPolys >> cptl;
03039 
03040       lcounts = new DWORD[nPolys];
03041 
03042       DWORDARRAY counts( lcounts, nPolys );
03043 
03044       ds >> counts;
03045 
03046       lpoints = new POINTL[cptl];
03047 
03048       POINTLARRAY points( lpoints, cptl );
03049 
03050       ds >> points;
03051     }
03055     bool serialize ( DATASTREAM ds )
03056     {
03057       ds << emr << rclBounds << nPolys << cptl << DWORDARRAY( lcounts, nPolys )
03058          << POINTLARRAY( lpoints, cptl );
03059       return true;
03060     }
03064     int size ( void ) const { return emr.nSize; }
03070     void execute ( METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT* source, HDC dc ) const
03071     {
03072       // According to the wine windef.h header, POINT and POINTL are equivalent
03073       // (but DWORD and INT are not)
03074       INT* counts = new INT[nPolys];
03075       for ( unsigned int i = 0; i < nPolys; i++ )
03076         counts[i] = lcounts[i];
03077 
03078       PolyPolygon( dc, (POINT*)lpoints, counts, nPolys );
03079 
03080       delete[] counts;
03081     }
03082 #ifdef ENABLE_EDITING
03083 
03086     void edit ( void ) const
03087     {
03088       printf( "*POLYPOLYGON*\n" );
03089       printf( "\trclBounds.left    : %ld\n", rclBounds.left );
03090       printf( "\trclBounds.top     : %ld\n", rclBounds.top );
03091       printf( "\trclBounds.right   : %ld\n", rclBounds.right );
03092       printf( "\trclBounds.bottom  : %ld\n", rclBounds.bottom );
03093       printf( "\tnPolys            : %ld\n", nPolys );
03094       printf( "\tcptl              : %ld\n", cptl );
03095       printf( "\taPolyCounts->\n" );
03096       for ( unsigned int i = 0; i < nPolys; i++ )
03097         printf( "\t\t%ld\n", lcounts[i] );
03098       printf( "\taptl->\n" );
03099       for ( unsigned int i = 0; i < cptl; i++ )
03100         printf( "\t\t%ld, %ld\n", lpoints[i].x, lpoints[i].y );
03101     }
03102 #endif /* ENABLE_EDITING */
03103   };
03104 
03106 
03109   class EMRPOLYBEZIER : public METARECORD, ::EMRPOLYBEZIER {
03110     POINTL* lpoints;
03111   public:
03117     EMRPOLYBEZIER ( const RECTL* bounds, const POINT* points, INT n )
03118     {
03119       cptl = n;
03120       aptl[0].x = 0;            // Really unused
03121       aptl[0].y = 0;
03122 
03123       emr.iType = EMR_POLYBEZIER;
03124       // The (cptl-1) below is to account for aptl, which isn't written out
03125       emr.nSize = sizeof( ::EMRPOLYBEZIER ) + sizeof( POINTL ) * (cptl-1);
03126 
03127       lpoints = new POINTL[cptl];
03128 
03129       for (int i=0; i<n; i++) {
03130         lpoints[i].x = points[i].x;
03131         lpoints[i].y = points[i].y;
03132       }
03133 
03134       rclBounds = *bounds;
03135     }
03140     EMRPOLYBEZIER ( DATASTREAM& ds )
03141     {
03142       ds >> emr >> rclBounds >> cptl;
03143 
03144       lpoints = new POINTL[cptl];
03145 
03146       POINTLARRAY points( lpoints, cptl );
03147 
03148       ds >> points;
03149     }
03153     ~EMRPOLYBEZIER ( )
03154     {
03155       if ( lpoints ) delete[] lpoints;
03156     }
03160     bool serialize ( DATASTREAM ds )
03161     {
03162       ds << emr << rclBounds << cptl << POINTLARRAY( lpoints, cptl );
03163       return true;
03164     }
03168     int size ( void ) const { return emr.nSize; }
03174     void execute ( METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT* source, HDC dc ) const
03175     {
03176       // According to the wine windef.h header, POINT and POINTL are equivalent
03177       PolyBezier( dc, (POINT*)lpoints, cptl );
03178     }
03179 #ifdef ENABLE_EDITING
03180 
03183     void edit ( void ) const
03184     {
03185       printf( "*POLYBEZIER*\n" );
03186       printf( "\trclBounds.left    : %ld\n", rclBounds.left );
03187       printf( "\trclBounds.top     : %ld\n", rclBounds.top );
03188       printf( "\trclBounds.right   : %ld\n", rclBounds.right );
03189       printf( "\trclBounds.bottom  : %ld\n", rclBounds.bottom );
03190       printf( "\tcptl              : %ld\n", cptl );
03191       printf( "\taptl->\n" );
03192       for ( unsigned int i = 0; i < cptl; i++ )
03193         printf( "\t\t%ld, %ld\n", lpoints[i].x, lpoints[i].y );
03194     }
03195 #endif /* ENABLE_EDITING */
03196   };
03197 
03199 
03202   class EMRPOLYBEZIER16 : public METARECORD, ::EMRPOLYBEZIER16 {
03203     POINT16* lpoints;
03204   public:
03210     EMRPOLYBEZIER16 ( const RECTL* bounds, const POINT16* points, INT n )
03211     {

03214       apts[0].y = 0;
03215

03217       // The (cptl-1) below is to account for aptl, which isn't written out

03221

03224         lpoints[i].y = points[i].y;
03225       }

03227       rclBounds = *bounds;
03228     }

03234     {

03236

03238

03240

03242     }

03247     {

03249     }
03253     bool serialize ( DATASTREAM ds )
03254     {
03255       ds << emr << rclBounds << cpts << POINT16ARRAY( lpoints, cpts );
03256       return true;
03257     }

03267     void execute ( METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT* source, HDC dc ) const
03268     {

03270       PolyBezier16( dc, lpoints, cpts );
03271     }

03276     void edit ( void ) const
03277     {
03278       printf( "*POLYBEZIER16*\n" );
03279       printf( "\trclBounds.left    : %ld\n", rclBounds.left );

03281       printf( "\trclBounds.right   : %ld\n", rclBounds.right );
03282       printf( "\trclBounds.bottom  : %ld\n", rclBounds.bottom );
03283       printf( "\tcpts              : %ld\n", cpts );
03284       printf( "\tapts->\n" );
03285       for ( unsigned int i = 0; i < cpts; i++ )

03287     }
03288 #endif /* ENABLE_EDITING */
